skill.name: competitive-landscape
skill.description: Analyze competitors, market positioning, differentiation, pricing, and strategic threats using structured business frameworks.
skill.summary: Build structured competitive landscape analysis for positioning, differentiation, pricing, and market strategy decisions.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: AI review found the static detections are false positives in documentation-only Markdown. Backticks are examples or reference file names, weak cryptography hits are substring matches, and no executable code, data exfiltration, prompt injection, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: False positive: Markdown backticks flagged as shell execution
low_findings.description: The flagged locations are Markdown code fences, ASCII diagrams, templates, and inline reference file names. They do not invoke Ruby, a shell, or any executable command path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each flagged backtick appears in Markdown documentation or inline file references. No surrounding text defines a script, runtime command, or user-controlled command execution path.

low_findings.title: False positive: Weak cryptography detector matched ordinary text
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain skill metadata and a product differentiation bullet. They do not reference DES, MD5, SHA-1, or any cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is business strategy prose, not software security logic. No cryptographic API, algorithm selection, hashing, or encryption behavior is present.

low_findings.title: False positive: System reconnaissance detector matched a pricing table
low_findings.description: The flagged line is a Markdown pricing comparison table header. It does not collect host details, enumerate system state, or run reconnaissance commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is visibly part of a competitor pricing matrix. There is no command, file access, environment access, or system information collection behavior.

content.value_statement: Competitive strategy work often lacks a clear structure and misses important market forces. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through repeatable frameworks for competitor analysis, positioning, pricing, and advantage mapping.
actual_capabilities: Applies Porter's Five Forces to assess industry attractiveness and competitive intensity.
Builds positioning maps using price, complexity, customer segment, or specialization dimensions.
Structures competitor profiles across product, pricing, go-to-market, strengths, weaknesses, and likely moves.
Guides Blue Ocean Strategy work using eliminate, reduce, raise, and create prompts.
Compares pricing tiers and packaging signals across competitors.
Defines monitoring cadence for product changes, market signals, and performance metrics.
limitations: Does not include live market data or automatic web research.
Requires users to provide competitor names, observations, or source material.
Does not validate financial claims, customer counts, or market share estimates.
Provides strategy analysis, not legal, investment, or financial advice.

use_cases.title: Evaluate a Startup Market
use_cases.description: Map direct, indirect, and future competitors before choosing a beachhead segment.

use_cases.title: Plan Product Positioning
use_cases.description: Identify white space, differentiation themes, and defensible advantages for a product launch.

use_cases.title: Improve Sales Battlecards
use_cases.description: Summarize competitor strengths, weaknesses, pricing signals, and likely objections for revenue teams.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Competitor Overview
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you have a short list of competitors and need a quick structure.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the competitive landscape for [company or product]. Compare these competitors: [list]. Cover target customers, core features, pricing approach, strengths, weaknesses, and clear differentiation opportunities.

prompt_templates.title: Five Forces Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when evaluating market attractiveness or investment in a category.
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply Porter's Five Forces to [market]. Score each force from 1 to 5, explain the score, identify the main evidence needed, and summarize whether the market looks attractive.

prompt_templates.title: Positioning Map and White Space
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when preparing positioning or launch strategy.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a positioning analysis for [product] against [competitors]. Choose two useful dimensions, place each competitor, explain the white space, and draft a positioning statement.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Competitive Strategy
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when building a full strategy review from source material.
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the provided notes, build a competitive strategy report for [company]. Include competitor profiles, Five Forces, Blue Ocean actions, pricing comparison, defensible advantages, monitoring cadence, and recommended next moves.

output_examples.input: Analyze our project management product against three enterprise competitors.
output_examples.output: A structured report with competitor profiles, a positioning map, Five Forces scores, pricing observations, and recommended differentiation themes.

output_examples.input: Find a beachhead segment for a new analytics startup.
output_examples.output: A segment comparison that scores customer pain, competition, willingness to pay, reachability, and expansion potential.

output_examples.input: Create a quarterly competitor monitoring plan.
output_examples.output: A monitoring cadence covering weekly release notes, monthly win-loss reviews, quarterly strategy updates, and annual market reassessment.

best_practices: Provide named competitors, target customers, pricing data, and recent market signals.
Separate confirmed facts from assumptions before asking for strategy recommendations.
Use the output as a decision framework, then validate key claims with primary sources.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for a final strategy without sharing competitor or market context.
Do not treat framework scores as precise market measurements.
Do not rely on competitor claims without checking source quality and recency.

faq.question: Does this skill research competitors automatically?
faq.answer: No. It organizes and analyzes information you provide, but it does not perform live web research by itself.

faq.question: Can it compare pricing and packaging?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a pricing comparison matrix and prompts for assessing market position and packaging gaps.

faq.question: Which strategy frameworks are included?
faq.answer: It covers Porter's Five Forces, Blue Ocean Strategy, positioning maps, competitor profiles, and advantage testing.

faq.question: Is this useful for early-stage startups?
faq.answer: Yes. Founders can use it to identify competitors, choose a beachhead market, and clarify differentiation.

faq.question: Can it create sales battlecards?
faq.answer: It can structure competitor strengths, weaknesses, objections, pricing signals, and positioning points for battlecard content.

faq.question: Does it provide investment advice?
faq.answer: No. It provides business strategy analysis and should not replace financial, legal, or investment advice.
